files.year_offset_format: uint32_t max_orderkey header + uint8_t[max_orderkey+1], value = year - 1992

storage_extensions.usage: mmap year_offset.bin; skip header (4 bytes); for qualifying lineitem row, year_off = year_data[l_orderkey] gives year_offset directly. Replaces orders_orderkey_lookup.bin (229MB) + o_orderdate.bin (57MB) + year_from_days() computation with single 57MB lookup.

join_order.notes: Bitset indexed by partkey-1 (0-based), partkeys contiguous 1..n_parts.
Use partsupp_pk_index dense_range, scan ~4 entries per partkey to match suppkey.

Use year_offset.bin storage extension: year_data[l_orderkey] gives year_offset directly. NO orders_orderkey_lookup.bin or o_orderdate.bin needed. Eliminates one level of indirection and year_from_days computation.
s_nationkey[suppkey - 1] gives nationkey directly (suppkey is 1-based contiguous).

filters.method: memmem on varlen_string offsets_plus_data, PARALLEL with OpenMP schedule(static)

data_structures.supplier_nationkey: Direct access: s_nationkey[suppkey - 1] gives nationkey. No separate array needed.
data_structures.year_lookup: uint8_t* from storage extension year_offset.bin, indexed by l_orderkey, gives year - 1992
data_structures.aggregation: thread-local double[25][8] direct 2D array (nation x year_offset), year_offset = year - 1992

parallelism.notes: CRITICAL OPTIMIZATIONS over iter_3: (1) STORAGE EXTENSION: Replace orders_orderkey_lookup.bin (229MB) + o_orderdate.bin (57MB) with year_offset.bin (57MB). Access: const uint8_t* year_data = (const uint8_t*)(mmap_year_offset.data + 4); then year_off = year_data[l_orderkey]. This eliminates one indirection level and year_from_days computation per qualifying row. (2) BLOCK-BASED SCAN WITH PREFETCHING: Process lineitem in blocks of 4096 rows. For each block: Pass 1 — scan l_partkey, collect qualifying indices into a local uint32_t sel_vec[225]. Pass 2 — for each qualifying index in sel_vec, issue __builtin_prefetch for: (a) &ps_index[l_partkey[sel[j]]], (b) &year_data[l_orderkey[sel[j]]], (c) &l_extendedprice[sel[j]], (d) &l_discount[sel[j]], (e) &l_quantity[sel[j]]. Pass 3 — process qualifying rows with prefetched data: lookup partsupp, read year_offset, read s_nationkey, compute amount, accumulate. The prefetch distance between Pass 2 and Pass 3 (~225 iterations × ~2ns = ~450ns) ensures L2/LLC hits instead of DRAM misses on the random-access columns. (3) PARALLEL PREFAULT: Same as iter_3 but exclude orders_orderkey_lookup and o_orderdate (no longer mmapped). Reduced working set: ~2.5GB → ~2.2GB → fewer pages to prefault → faster. (4) MADVISE TUNING: MADV_RANDOM only on ps_suppkey and ps_supplycost. MADV_SEQUENTIAL on year_offset.bin (semi-sequential via l_orderkey sort). All lineitem columns keep default (sequential). (5) Keep schedule(static) for all parallel loops.

indexes.purpose: partsupp lookup by l_partkey, scan ~4 entries for matching l_suppkey

optimization_notes: Root cause: Category B+E. prefault_pages=23ms and main_scan=27ms together account for 93% of 54ms. The main_scan is latency-limited by random cache misses: each of 3.3M qualifying rows requires ~5 dependent random accesses (ps_index, ps_suppkey, ps_supplycost, orders_lookup, o_orderdate) plus year_from_days computation. Two fixes: (E) Built a storage extension orders.orderkey_year.direct_lookup — a dense uint8_t[max_orderkey+1] array mapping orderkey directly to year_offset, eliminating orders_orderkey_lookup.bin (229MB) and o_orderdate.bin (57MB) and year_from_days(). This removes 229MB from the mmap working set and eliminates one level of pointer indirection per qualifying row. (B) Block-based scan with software prefetching — process lineitem in 4096-row blocks with a selection vector: Pass 1 filters l_partkey via bitset, Pass 2 issues prefetches for all random-access targets of qualifying rows, Pass 3 processes qualifying rows with data already in cache. The prefetch-to-use distance (~225 qualifying rows × ~2ns compute each ≈ 450ns) covers the ~100-200ns DRAM latency. Together these reduce random access latency in main_scan and shrink the prefault working set.
